title: Home Page
text:
Hello, my name is Tadeh Peroomian. I am an undergraduate student at The University of California Santa Barbara(UCSB), majoring in Environmental Studies (B.S.). This website serves as a portfolio of the academic knowledge and professional skills I have gained as a UCSB Student.
Currently, I am searching for research opportunities in the fields of Environmental Studies and Marine Biology at UCSB in order to gain new skills, meet new people, and prepare myself for Graduate School.

title: About Me
text: As I entered my first year of college as a Biology major, I had plans to become a Veterinarian. I faced many hardships in that first year, and felt as though I did not enjoy what I was studying. Thus, over the course of that year, I changed my major multiple times trying to find something new that I loved. However, after a field studies program in Baja California for Marine Biology, I fell back in love with the subject, and applied to UCSB as an Environmental Studies major.

title: About Me
text: As I entered my first year of college as a Biology major, I had plans to become a Veterinarian. I faced many hardships in that first year, and felt as though I did not enjoy what I was studying. Thus, over the course of that year, I changed my major multiple times trying to find something new that I loved. However, after a field studies program in Baja California for Marine Biology, I fell back in love with the subject, and applied to UCSB as an Environmental Studies major.

title: About Me
text:
Education
As a third year student at UCSB, I have taken quite a few upper division Environmental Studies classes that have better prepared me for work in the field, including:

ENVS 171 – Ecosystem Processes
ENVS 127A – Foundations of Environmental Education
ENVS 127B – Advanced Environmental Education and Practicum
ENVS 193DS – Statistics for Environmental Science

Before transferring to UCSB, I attended Glendale Community College, from where I earned an A.A. in Liberal Arts: Science and Mathematics Emphasis.

title: About Me
text:
Professional Development
In my time as a college student, I have had the opportunity to participate in internships and work experiences.
I spent a year as an intern in the Malacology Department of the Los Angeles Natural History Museum, researching the Armina californica sea slug, performing PCR testing, Gel Electrophoresis, and comparing genetics through various software.
I also worked as a Planetarium Presenter at the Glendale Community College Planetarium, where I designed my own presentation and presented them to students from the local elementary and middle schools, guiding them through the Universe.
